newsletters.subject: How to run Claude Cowork from your phone
newsletters.summary: Workflow guide on Claude Dispatch — running Cowork tasks asynchronously from phone to desktop. Covers the two-minute phone-desktop pairing setup, how Dispatch differs from normal chatbot usage (asynchronous orchestrator vs. interactive chat), and weekly prompt patterns.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI Agent workflows, Claude Cowork ecosystem, mobile agent interaction patterns
newsletters.recommended_action: Enrich entities: claude-cowork, dispatch. Update claude-cowork entity page with mobile/remote usage patterns.

newsletters.subject: Building gets easier
newsletters.summary: Comprehensive roundup of agentic AI tooling: Cloudflare now allows agents to create accounts, buy domains, and deploy services autonomously; Stripe agentic commerce suite with Link wallet for agents (one-time-use credentials via CLI); Cursor SDK for building agents on same runtime as Cursor editor; Warp terminal going open-source with OpenAI as founding sponsor; Poolside releasing Laguna M.1 and XS.2 models for agentic coding; here.now agent cloud storage; Sandcastle open-source software factory; Martin Fowler's updated AI coding notes on vibe coding vs agentic engineering.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI coding agents, agent infrastructure, agentic commerce, developer tooling evolution
newsletters.recommended_action: Create/enrich entities: cloudflare-agents, stripe-agentic-commerce, cursor-sdk, warp-terminal, poolside-laguna, here-now, sandcastle, agentic-engineering. Update ben-tossell entity. High-value content for agent infrastructure pages.

newsletters.subject: $700 Billion AI Bet, One Earnings Night
newsletters.summary: Big Tech Q1 2026 earnings analysis: combined AI capex now exceeding $700B for 2026. Google Cloud surged 63% to $20B (first time AI enterprise tools became #1 growth driver), shares +6%. Microsoft capex forecast $190B, Azure supply-constrained. Meta capex up to $145B, shares -10% on safety backlash. Amazon stable at $200B. Futurum Group analysis confirms $660-690B hyperscaler spend, Stargate $500B infrastructure project (OpenAI/SoftBank/Oracle), pure-play AI revenue <$35B vs capex gap, China investing ~$76B (Alibaba $53B/3yr, ByteDance $23B).
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI infrastructure economics, hyperscaler capex, AI industry financial landscape
newsletters.recommended_action: Create concept page: ai-infrastructure-economics or update existing hyperscaler/investment pages. Key data for understanding the economic forces driving AI agent deployment.

newsletters.subject: True Positive Weekly #159
newsletters.summary: Weekly AI/ML news roundup: AI revolution in math (Quanta Magazine), hidden cost of Google's AI defaults (Ars Technica), developing future-ready skills with generative AI (Google Research), 8 tips for writing agent skills (Phil Schmid blog), visualizing loss landscapes of neural networks (Hackerstreak), Gemma 4 fine-tuning guide (Unsloth), Dive into Claude Code (ChapterPal podcast on agent system design space).
newsletters.wiki_relevance: Agent skills best practices, Claude Code, Gemma 4, ML visualization
newsletters.recommended_action: Create concept page: agent-skills-writing (based on Phil Schmid's 8 tips). Enrich gemma-4 entity with fine-tuning info. ChapterPal Claude Code content valuable for claude-code entity.

newsletters.subject: AI Value Capture - The Shift To Model Labs
newsletters.summary: Deep analysis of AI value capture shift from infrastructure (Nvidia/TSMC) to model labs (Anthropic/OpenAI). Key findings: Anthropic ARR exploded from $9B to $44B, gross margins on inference up from 38% to 70%. Agentic AI transformed tokens from chat outputs to labor replacements. Cache hit rates 90%+ bring effective cost to ~$0.99/M tokens despite high sticker prices. Software optimizations (wideEP + disagg + MTP) yield 14x throughput increase. Vera Rubin VR NVL72 launching 2H26 with SOCAMM socketed memory. GPU rental economics: cost floor $4.92/hr vs value ceiling $12.25/hr. Nvidia acting as 'Central Bank' — strategic pricing restraint to maintain ecosystem dependence.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI economics, token economics, agentic AI ROI, GPU infrastructure pricing, model lab business models
newsletters.recommended_action: Create concept page: ai-value-capture-economics. Enrich semianalysis entity. Key data for understanding the economic viability of AI agents at scale.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] Agents for Everything Else: Codex for Knowledge Work, Claude for Creative Work
newsletters.summary: Comprehensive frontier AI landscape update. Codex: pivoted from dev tool to 'SuperApp' for knowledge work, 42% faster Computer Use, dynamic UI routing, /chronicle and /goal commands, deep Microsoft/Google/Salesforce integrations. Claude: creative suite support (Blender, Autodesk, Adobe, Ableton, Canva), Claude Security with Opus 4.7, 1M conversation study on sycophancy. GPT-5.5 cyber: 71.4% pass rate on UK AI Security Institute evals (vs Claude Mythos 68.6%), GPT-5.5 Pro 60% lower cost on CritPt. Open models: Qwen3.6 27B (Apache 2.0, 262K context, single H100), Grok 4.3 (1500 Elo), Hy3-preview (295B MoE), Ling 2.6 1T. DeepSeek V4-Flash: outputs bounding boxes/coordinates for GUI navigation. Pretraining now >100T tokens. Cursor agent harness engineering details. PyPI lightning package supply chain attack.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: Frontier model benchmarks, Codex expansion, Claude capabilities, open-source models, agent infrastructure, AI security
newsletters.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY: Create/update entities: codex-superapp, gpt-5-5, claude-mythos, qwen3-6, grok-4-3, hy3-preview, deepseek-v4-flash, cursor-agent-harness. Create concept: agent-harness-engineering. Update swyx entity.
